{"step_by_step_thinking": "The McRoberts maneuver involves flexing the thigh over the abdomen. This maneuver is used to assess for sciatic nerve irritation or compression. The common peroneal nerve, which is a branch of the sciatic nerve, is involved in foot and ankle movements and sensation in the lateral leg and dorsum of the foot. Given the nature of the maneuver and the involvement of the sciatic nerve, the common peroneal nerve is the most likely nerve to be involved.", "answer_choice": "A"}
